payload.text: I read this as research for a browser-shooter design spec - I’ll verify the current range layout and Soldier: 76 mechanics against official and community references, then return a cited markdown summary.

I read this as a researched implementation spec - below is a concise, cited reference for a single-player browser clone.

# Overwatch Practice Range + Soldier: 76 Reference Spec

**Scope:** Current standard/core gameplay reference checked July 17, 2026. Excludes Stadium items, seasonal event mutations, and optional mid-match Perks unless explicitly noted.

## 1. Practice Range layout

### Overall structure and objective
- Industrial Overwatch training facility; a free-form sandbox with **no match win condition**.
- Primary purposes: swap heroes, test abilities, calibrate aim/settings, practice movement, and charge/use ultimates.
- The range contains **nine friendly** and **nineteen hostile** training bots, including a configurable Hero Bot. Ground distance markings are provided. [1]

### Player spawn / hub
- Start in a safe spawn room facing stairs down into the defense area.
- **Left of spawn:** elevated **Hero Bot plateau** with one configurable enemy Hero Bot, two static hostile bots, an ultimate-charge pickup, and a jump pad to otherwise inaccessible high ground.
- **Right of spawn:** route to moving-target aim section and enclosed firing-range simulator.
- Include a hero-change terminal near the firing range; a clone can make it accessible from the pause/loadout UI instead.

### Target and combat zones
| Zone | Layout / targets | Clone purpose |
|---|---|---|
| **Hero Bot plateau** | Configurable enemy Hero Bot; static bots across the platform; high ground reachable by jump pad. | Duel target, ability testing, vertical-movement target. |
| **Defense portion** | Main open yard below spawn: friendly and hostile bots exchange fire; a friendly hero shoots a shielded tank bot; two hostile/friendly bot pairs can be switched to rocket/heavy variants. Includes health packs, an elevated sniper bot, a moving platform with a static bot, and an ultimate pickup. | Mixed combat, cover, healing, explosive and shield testing. |
| **Aiming section** | Three standard/friendly bots plus one heavy/tank bot move continuously in looping patterns. Nearby control swaps friendly/hostile state. | Tracking aim against horizontally moving targets. |
| **Firing range** | Enclosed simulation room. Configure bot vs. target props, movement pattern (including random), and distance; the room labels distances through **40 m**. Score appears top-left, selected settings top-right; an ultimate pickup remains usable. | Repeatable aim drill: static, lateral, vertical, random movement, and distance tests. |
| **Environmental rooms** | Balcony, gym with weights/punching bags/lockers, non-combat walkways, health-pack platforms, moving platforms, high-ground routes. | Sell the training-facility setting; provide traversal and splash-damage surfaces, not objectives. |

### Recommended browser-clone minimum
1. Spawn/hub with loadout reset.
2. Open firing lane with **static targets at 5 m increments from 5-40 m** (the exact 5 m subdivision is a clone convenience; the source only establishes meter markers and a 40 m range).
3. Adjacent looping lateral-movement lane.
4. Raised platform / cover / jump pad for jump and rocket testing.
5. Resettable score counter, hit markers, cooldown reset, and ultimate-charge pickup.
6. Optional hostile bots should shoot slow projectiles so Biotic Field and movement have a purpose.

## 2. Soldier: 76 core kit

### Heavy Pulse Rifle - primary fire
- **Type:** fully automatic **hitscan** rifle; no projectile lead.
- **Magazine:** 30 rounds.
- **Fire rate:** 9 rounds/second.
- **Reload:** 1.5 s animation; ammo is restored about 0.736 s into it.
- **Damage:** 19 body damage per shot before falloff; critical hits are supported.
- **Falloff:** full damage through 30 m, then linearly falls to **5.7** damage at 50 m (30% minimum).
- **Implementation:** raycast once per firing interval; apply head-hit multiplier where supported; use upward recoil rather than random spread. [2][3]

### Sprint
- **Effect:** indefinite forward-only sprint at **+50%** movement speed: normal 5.5 m/s -> **8.25 m/s**.
- **Cancel conditions:** release/toggle Sprint off, stop moving forward, or take an action other than running forward (notably primary fire, Helix Rockets, and ability use).
- **Recovery:** firing cannot resume for **0.3 s** after Sprint ends.
- **Current exception:** reloading is permitted while Sprinting. During that reload the Sprint bonus is halved and then diminishes over the reload. [2][4]
- **Clone behavior:** do not permit firing while sprinting; retain the 0.3 s exit lockout so Sprint is movement repositioning rather than a no-cost DPS boost.

### Helix Rockets - secondary fire
- **Type:** one launched volley rendered as **three spiraling mini-rockets**; it is a travel-time projectile, not hitscan.
- **Cooldown:** 6 s.
- **Projectile speed:** 50 m/s.
- **Direct damage:** 40 per mini-rocket, **120 total** for the full direct volley.
- **Explosion:** enemy splash damage is **80** at the inner radius, falling to **40** at 3 m; falloff begins at 1 m.
- **Self damage:** Soldier takes 50% of splash damage and receives explosion knockback.
- **Physics:** straight flight along aim direction; collision detonates in a spherical AoE; no headshots; apply knockback. [2][5]

### Biotic Field
- **Effect:** deploy an invulnerable stationary emitter that heals Soldier and allies in radius.
- **Heal rate:** **40 HP/s**.
- **Duration:** 5 s.
- **Radius:** 5 m.
- **Cooldown:** 15 s.
- **Semantics:** one active field at once; targets need line-of-sight to the emitter, but enemy barriers do not block the field's healing. [2]
- **Clone behavior:** emit visible healing pulses and a ground-radius decal; healing ends immediately when a target leaves the radius.

### Tactical Visor - ultimate
- **Duration:** 6 s.
- **Activation:** instantly refills rifle ammo; primary-fire reload is reduced to 0.5 s while active.
- **Auto-aim:** primary fire locks toward the valid enemy closest to the reticle, within a **45-degree** targeting cone and **45 m** range. Target center mass must be visible; barriers block a lock.
- **Does not:** increase damage, remove normal falloff, or auto-aim Helix Rockets.
- **Critical-hit rule:** body auto-aim does not create artificial headshots; a shot only crits when it would have naturally contacted the head. [2]
- **Clone UI:** visor overlay, target-lock reticle, target-switch cue on loss of line of sight, and distinct ultimate activation VO/flash.

## 3. Animation requirements

| State | Required transitions / details |
|---|---|
| **Idle** | Relaxed armed stance, breathing/weapon sway; transition to aiming/fire. |
| **Run** | Standard forward locomotion with weapon carried; strafing/backpedal variants if third-person is visible. |
| **Sprint** | Distinct lowered-rifle forward run; exit into the 0.3 s recovery before fire. |
| **Jump / land** | Takeoff, airborne loop, landing; preserve directional momentum. |
| **Primary fire** | Fast looping recoil/muzzle-flash cycle at 9 rounds/s; ammo counter and hit feedback per shot. |
| **Reload** | Magazine/weapon manipulation; refill gameplay ammo at 0.736 s, finish visual at 1.5 s. |
| **Helix Rockets** | Brief rifle launch/recovery animation, three corkscrewing trails, explosion, and self-knockback response. |
| **Biotic Field cast** | Place/throw emitter to ground, arm it, begin pulsing healing volume. |
| **Ultimate** | Visor activation gesture/overlay, instant ammo refill, lock-on reticle behavior for 6 s, deactivation. |
| **Hit / death** | Directional hit flinch or camera kick; death collapse/ragdoll, respawn/reset state. |

## 4. Visual references

- **Practice Range overview:** [Practice Range.jpg](https://static.wikia.nocookie.net/overwatch_gamepedia/images/1/1a/Practice_Range.jpg/revision/latest?cb=20190512035034)
- **Moving-target aiming section:** [Aiming portion](https://static.wikia.nocookie.net/overwatch_gamepedia/images/f/ff/Aiming_portion.png/revision/latest?cb=20250228160655)
- **Enclosed firing-range simulation:** [Firing range in use](https://static.wikia.nocookie.net/overwatch_gamepedia/images/e/e7/Firing_range_in_use.png/revision/latest?cb=20250228161318)
- **Defense-area overhead view:** [Defense portion](https://static.wikia.nocookie.net/overwatch_gamepedia/images/8/85/View_from_above_the_defence_portion_of_the_practice_range.png/revision/latest?cb=20250228160045)
- **Official Soldier: 76 hero page:** [Blizzard - Soldier: 76](https://overwatch.blizzard.com/en-us/heroes/soldier-76/)

## Sources

1. [Overwatch Wiki - Practice Range](https://overwatch.fandom.com/wiki/Practice_Range) - map sections, bot counts/types, firing-range controls, pickups, and screenshots.  
2. [Overwatch Wiki - Soldier: 76](https://overwatch.fandom.com/wiki/Soldier:_76) - current detailed core-kit values and mechanics.  
3. [Blizzard patch notes - March 2025](https://overwatch.blizzard.com/en-us/news/patch-notes/live/2025/3/) - confirms the current Heavy Pulse Rifle hitbox update.  
4. [Blizzard patch notes - April 2026](https://overwatch.blizzard.com/en-us/news/patch-notes/live/2026/4/) - confirms reload-while-Sprinting and its speed penalty.  
5. [Blizzard patch notes - February 2026](https://overwatch.blizzard.com/en-us/news/patch-notes/live/2026/2/) - confirms Helix Rockets' three 40-damage mini-rocket presentation and current perk changes.
